violations.finding: Comment references decimal step number 'Step 3.5' in: '# Step 3.5 MUST complete before clean-inspect (Step 4)'
violations.recommendation: Remove decimal step reference or update to integer form: '# Rule inspection MUST complete before clean-inspect' or '# Step 4 MUST complete before clean-inspect (Step 5)' — whichever matches the current step numbering in commands/clean.md

violations.finding: Hardcoded relative directory path 'docs/clean/rule-context-${REQUEST_ID}.json' — the 'docs/clean' subdirectory is not derived from PROJECT_ROOT (which is extracted from the context JSON at line 190) or any script parameter
Hardcoded numeric constant MAX_ITERATIONS=5 with no parameter override mechanism (same literal value is also emitted in the JSON output at line 145: max_iterations: 5)
violations.recommendation: Use environment variable with default: MAX_ITERATIONS="${MAX_ITERATIONS:-5}"; then pass it to jq via --argjson max_iterations "$MAX_ITERATIONS" so the JSON output stays consistent

violations.finding: Hardcoded folder list KEY_FOLDERS=("agents" "scripts" "docs" "hooks" "commands") — the set of key folders is a project-specific configuration value that is not derived from the context JSON's full_context.discovered_folders field, even though PROJECT_ROOT is already read from that same context file
violations.recommendation: Derive folder list from context: KEY_FOLDERS=($(jq -r '.full_context.discovered_folders // [] | .[]' "$CONTEXT_FILE")), or accept KEY_FOLDERS as an environment variable override

violations.finding: Hardcoded directory path 'docs/dev/overnight/' — script only accepts optional [session_id] argument and has no mechanism to specify a different project root or state directory, making it only usable from one specific working directory
violations.recommendation: Derive state directory from environment or git root: STATE_DIR="${OVERNIGHT_STATE_DIR:-${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)}/docs/dev/overnight}"; then use find "$STATE_DIR" ...

violations.finding: Hardcoded environment-specific absolute fallback path '/root' in resolve_project_dir() — this path is host-specific and will silently produce a wrong project dir on any non-root deployment
violations.recommendation: Remove the '/root' fallback; the function already tries CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR and pwd before reaching it. Use: git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd (fail with cwd rather than a wrong absolute path)

standards_passed: no-inline-code (all 5 files are shell scripts, not .md command/agent files)
use-source-venv (no python3 direct calls found in any file)
meaningful-naming (no enhance-*, fast-*, optimize-*, -v2, -v3, improved-*, better-*, new-* patterns)
english-only (no Chinese characters found in any file)
merge-similar-scripts (refine-context.sh and orchestrator.sh iterate() serve different callers and interfaces; plan-style-inspection.sh has no near-duplicate)
concise-documentation (all 5 files are shell scripts with lean comments, no verbose markdown)
dangling-references (no cross-script references to non-existent scripts found)
merge-conflicts (no conflict markers found in any file)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 4 actionable issues: (1) Finding 1 recommendation was imprecise about step number mapping — applied improved wording; (2) Finding 3 location was incomplete (line 145 also hardcodes the value 5) — extended location; (3) Findings 4 and 5 were duplicative with conflicting fixes — consolidated into one finding with unified recommendation; (4) Missed violation: KEY_FOLDERS hardcoded at line 202 — added as new finding. Codex also flagged /tmp paths at lines 25-27 etc. as a potential violation — rejected as out-of-scope: /tmp with timestamp-derived names is standard system temp file practice, not a project-specific hardcoded path. Codex raised a scope concern about changed-files-only vs full audit — rejected: the dispatch prompt explicitly requests full audit of the listed files with no --changed-files parameter.

findings.issue: Finding 1 recommendation imprecise — 'Step 4' may conflict with numbering in commands/clean.md
findings.proposed_fix: Change recommendation to: 'Change line 198 to # Step 4 MUST complete before clean-inspect (Step 5) or remove step numbers entirely: # Rule inspection MUST complete before clean-inspect'
findings.rationale: Recommendation updated to be more precise and cross-reference aware

findings.issue: Finding 3 incomplete — hardcoded value 5 also appears in JSON output at line 145
findings.proposed_fix: Extend location to scripts/orchestrator.sh:140,145 and extend fix to cover the jq JSON output
findings.rationale: Both occurrences of the hardcoded literal should be fixed together

findings.issue: Missed critical violation: KEY_FOLDERS hardcoded at orchestrator.sh:202
findings.proposed_fix: Add no-hardcoded-values finding for KEY_FOLDERS array
findings.rationale: Valid new finding — folder list is project-specific config that should be derived from context JSON

findings.issue: Findings 4 and 5 are duplicative with conflicting fix suggestions (different positional arg strategies)
findings.proposed_fix: Consolidate into one finding using env-var derivation approach: STATE_DIR derived from OVERNIGHT_STATE_DIR or CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR or git root
findings.rationale: Single consolidated finding is clearer and avoids interface-breaking recommendation

findings.issue: Finding 6 recommendation weak — pwd is already tried before /root fallback
findings.proposed_fix: Recommend removing /root fallback entirely: use git root or cwd, not a host-specific absolute path
findings.rationale: Stronger recommendation makes the intent clearer

findings.issue: /tmp hardcoded paths at orchestrator.sh:25-27 etc. flagged as potential no-hardcoded-values violations
findings.proposed_fix: N/A — Codex proposed TMP_DIR env var override
findings.rationale: /tmp with timestamp-derived unique suffixes is standard POSIX temp file practice, not a project-specific configurable path. Standard 2 targets paths like data/plain_text_resume.yaml or docs/dev/overnight/ — not system temp dirs.

findings.issue: Scope concern: none of the 5 scripts appear in current git diff, so all findings may be pre-existing
findings.proposed_fix: Move findings to pre-existing/out-of-scope section or explicitly define scope as full audit
findings.rationale: Dispatch prompt explicitly orders full audit of listed files with no --changed-files parameter; pre-existing violations are in scope for this invocation type

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied 5 of 7 codex findings: improved finding 1 recommendation, extended finding 3 location to include line 145, added new finding for KEY_FOLDERS hardcoded array, consolidated overnight-status.sh findings 4+5 into one, strengthened finding 6 recommendation. Rejected /tmp-as-hardcoded-path (system temp dir pattern is not Standard 2 violation) and the changed-files scope restriction (not applicable to this full-audit invocation).
